description: Dual H-bridge motor driver — 2.7V–10.8V, 1.5A/channel, integrated decay modes, fault output, WSON-16 or HTSSOP-16

pins.name: AOUT2
pins.description: H-bridge A output 2. Connect to motor A terminal.
pins.requirements: No external flyback diodes needed — internal diodes included

pins.name: VCP
pins.description: Charge pump capacitor pin. Connect 100nF cap from VCP to VM.
pins.requirements: 100nF cap from VCP to VM required for internal charge pump operation

pins.name: VM
pins.requirements: 100nF ceramic cap to GND close to pin.

pins.name: AOUT1
pins.description: H-bridge A output 1. Connect to other motor A terminal.

pins.name: GND
pins.requirements: Exposed pad on WSON must also be connected to GND.

pins.name: BIN2
pins.description: Logic input for H-bridge B, input 2.
pins.requirements: 3.3V and 5V logic compatible. Do not leave floating.

pins.name: BIN1
pins.description: Logic input for H-bridge B, input 1.

pins.name: nFAULT
pins.description: Fault indicator. Active LOW open-drain output.
pins.requirements: Pull-up resistor (10kΩ) to logic supply required.

pins.name: nSLEEP
pins.description: Sleep mode. Active LOW. Pull HIGH to enable device.
pins.requirements: Pull HIGH to Vcc for normal operation. Floating = device sleeps.

pins.name: AIN1
pins.description: Logic input for H-bridge A, input 1.

pins.name: AIN2
pins.description: Logic input for H-bridge A, input 2.

pins.name: VCC
pins.description: Logic supply voltage. 3.3V or 5V. Powers input logic.

pins.name: AISEN
pins.description: Current sense for H-bridge A. Short to GND if not used.

required_passives.reason: Charge pump capacitor — required for internal bootstrap operation
Pull-up required for open-drain nFAULT output

application_circuits.name: Dual DC motor control (3.3V/5V logic, 2.7–10.8V motor)
application_circuits.description: VM: motor supply. VCC: 3.3V or 5V logic. AIN1/AIN2 for motor A, BIN1/BIN2 for motor B. nSLEEP HIGH to enable. nFAULT with pull-up.

common_mistakes: nSLEEP must be pulled HIGH for device to operate — leaving it floating keeps device in sleep mode silently
nFAULT requires external pull-up resistor — open drain output with no internal pull-up
VCP (pin 2) requires 100nF cap to VM — not to GND — missing cap causes gate drive failure
AISEN and BISEN must not be left floating — short to GND if current sensing not used
VM max is 10.8V — do NOT use with 12V motor supplies (use L298N or DRV8701 for higher voltages)
Exposed pad on WSON package must be soldered to GND copper pour for thermal dissipation
Internal diodes are included — no external flyback diodes needed (unlike L298N)
notes: Much more efficient than L298N — lower dropout, integrated diodes, smaller package. VM range 2.7–10.8V makes it ideal for 3.3V–9V motor systems. Current limit: 1.5A continuous per channel. For 12V motor systems use L298N or TB6612FNG. nFAULT and nSLEEP are MCU-controllable for power management.
